name: Polyclonal IgG Antibody (generic, dilute)
cas_note: Antibodies are biologics; no CAS number applies. This entry represents a generic ~0.1–1 mg/mL polyclonal IgG in PBS + 0.1% BSA, 50% glycerol storage buffer.
vendor_example: Jackson ImmunoResearch 111-005-003 (normal goat IgG reference), Abcam ab37355
striking_fact: Dilute antibodies adsorb irreversibly to standard plastic — use LoBind tubes and never let concentration drop below 10 µg/mL without adding BSA carrier.

sds_facts.storage: Store at −20°C for long-term, or 4°C for up to 4 weeks. Aliquot before first freeze to avoid repeated freeze-thaw. 50% glycerol storage buffer prevents freezing at −20°C.
incompatibilities: azide-free formulations should not contain sodium azide if used with HRP conjugates

bench_knowledge: Aliquot before first thaw: divide into single-use volumes so the working aliquot is never refrozen. Freeze-thaw cycles aggregate IgG, reducing titer and creating non-specific staining artifacts.
Use LoBind (low-protein-binding) polypropylene tubes for all dilutions below ~0.5 mg/mL. Standard PP and PS tubes adsorb significant antibody from dilute solutions within 30–60 minutes.
Add BSA or another carrier protein (0.1–1% BSA) to very dilute working stocks (< 10 µg/mL) to occupy adsorption sites. Gelatin or casein work as alternatives if BSA interferes with your assay.
Never vortex antibody stocks — shear force at the air-water interface denatures IgG and creates aggregates. Mix by gentle inversion or flicking.
Keep on ice during use; do not leave at room temperature for more than 1–2 hours during extended staining procedures.
Spin briefly (1 min, 10,000 × g) before use if the antibody has been frozen — this pellets any aggregates that have formed and removes them from the working stock.
